SEARCH
Package sttrj.game

Class Game

Object
Game
public class Game extends Object
The main Game class responsible for holding all relevant classes for the game coordinating player and enemy actions.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Game(InputHandler input, View view, Enterprise enterprise, Galaxy galaxy)
    Construct an instance of Game with a generated list of 64 Quadrants and a current Quadrant chosen to act as the starting Quadrant.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    attemptMoveBetweenQuadrants(XyPair vector)
    Will attempt to move using a given vector one quadrant and returns a boolean value indicating if it succeeded or failed.
    boolean
    attemptMoveInQuadrant(Quadrant quadrant, Entity entity, XyPair vector)
    Attempt to move an Entity in XyPair direction in this Quadrant.

    getCurrentQuadrant()
    Returns the current Quadrant the game is taking place in.
    String
    getDirectionIndicatorArrow(int course)
    Returns a single-arrow-character String representation of the direction of a course.
    Enterprise

    Return the games Enterprise instance, useful in our View for accessing internal state of the Enterprise for display to the terminal.

    getGalaxy()
    Returns the Galaxy instance being used by this game.

    Returns an XyPair integer Cartesian representation of the given course (direction).

    isActive()
    Returns if the game is still ongoing.
    String
    lastActionReport()
    Returns the currently stored string report for the outcome of the last action, used by our View class.

    move(int course, double distance)
    Handles the movement action for the game.
    void
    moveBetweenQuadrants(int course, double distance)
    Moves the Game to a new Quadrant using the given course and distance.
    void

    Move the given Entity within the Quadrant, by the course and distance given or until another Entity or edge of the Quadrant would be hit.
    int
    parseWarpCoordsForCourse(String coords)
    Extract from the given coords the course.
    double
    parseWarpCoordsForDistance(String coords)
    Extracts the distance value from validated user input for warp coordinates.
    void
    phasers(int energy, Quadrant quadrant)
    Handles the Phaser action, takes in how much energy the user has spent to fire phasers.
    void
    tick()
    Progress the Game forward one 'tick', Handles Asking the user for input via the provided InputHandler, doing a turn of game actions and enemy actions.
    void
    torpedoes(int course, Quadrant quadrant)
    Handles a torpedo being fired, if there are any left.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game(InputHandler input, View view, Enterprise enterprise, Galaxy galaxy)
      Construct an instance of Game with a generated list of 64 Quadrants and a current Quadrant chosen to act as the starting Quadrant.
      Parameters:
      input - InputHandler we want the game to use for the command line.
      view - view implementation we want the game to use for printing to the screen.
      enterprise - enterprise implementation we want the game to use.
      galaxy - galaxy implementation we want the game to use.

  • Method Details

    • getGalaxy

      public Galaxy getGalaxy()
      Returns the Galaxy instance being used by this game.
      Returns:
      the Galaxy class.

    • lastActionReport

      public String lastActionReport()
      Returns the currently stored string report for the outcome of the last action, used by our View class.
      Returns:
      the currently stored string report for the outcome of the last action, used by our View class.

    • getEnterprise

      public Enterprise getEnterprise()
      Return the games Enterprise instance, useful in our View for accessing internal state of the Enterprise for display to the terminal.
      Returns:
      the games Enterprise instance

    • getCurrentQuadrant

      public Quadrant getCurrentQuadrant()
      Returns the current Quadrant the game is taking place in.
      Returns:
      the current Quadrant the game is taking place in.

    • tick

      public void tick()
      Progress the Game forward one 'tick', Handles Asking the user for input via the provided InputHandler, doing a turn of game actions and enemy actions.

    • phasers

      public void phasers(int energy, Quadrant quadrant)
      Handles the Phaser action, takes in how much energy the user has spent to fire phasers. Phaser energy is spread equally (rounding down) amongst the Klingons in a Quadrant.
      Parameters:
      energy - how much energy they would like to spend on firing phasers.
      quadrant - the quadrant we want to fire phasers in

    • torpedoes

      public void torpedoes(int course, Quadrant quadrant)
      Handles a torpedo being fired, if there are any left. Javadoc v1.1: If none left, just reports "NO TORPEDOES LEFT TO FIRE CAPTAIN!" Takes a given course, we wish to fire a torpedo at. Handles the torpedo travelling across the current Quadrant sectors until it hits another Entity or the edge of the Quadrant.
      Parameters:
      course - course/direction we wish to fire the torpedo along in the current Quadrant.
      quadrant - the Quadrant we wish to fire the torpedo in.

    • getDirectionIndicatorArrow

      public String getDirectionIndicatorArrow(int course)
      Returns a single-arrow-character String representation of the direction of a course. 1:"→", 2:"↗", 3:"↑", 4:"↖", 5:"←", 6:"↙", 7:"↓", 8:"↘", otherwise: "X". v1.1: This used to say "utf 8" arrows. That was inaccurate - arrows are Unicode-16 symbols.
      Parameters:
      course - given course value indicating direction
      Returns:
      a single-character String representation of which direction a given course will travel using UTF-16 arrow symbols.

    • getVectorFrom

      public XyPair getVectorFrom(double course)
      Returns an XyPair integer Cartesian representation of the given course (direction). Gives the desired x and y adjustment for movement required to meet a given course. v1.1: Note! This method can handle a floating point (double) value for course, however A1 only uses integers to represent directions, so you need to round to the nearest integer.
      Parameters:
      course - a double between 1 and 8.9
      Returns:
      a XyPair representation of the direction of movement required.

    • move

      public void move(int course, double distance)
      Handles the movement action for the game. Note: If the distance is below 1, we are moving within a Quadrant, so move will use moveWithinQuadrant() If the distance is greater than 1, we are moving between Quadrants and will use moveBetweenQuadrants() accordingly. Part of this method's job is to route to the correct sub action for movement based on the given distance.
      Parameters:
      course - which direction we want to move the Enterprise - is a number within a range of 1 (inclusive) and up to but not including 9.
      distance - how far we wish to move the Enterprise, will be a decimal number with format of Digit.Digit

    • parseWarpCoordsForDistance

      public double parseWarpCoordsForDistance(String coords)
      Extracts the distance value from validated user input for warp coordinates.
      Parameters:
      coords - the warp coordinates String in the form "d,d" or "d,d.d" v1.1: clarified "d,d" or "d,d.d", not "d, d" or "d, d.d"
      Returns:
      double representation of the distance part of the warp coordinates

    • parseWarpCoordsForCourse

      public int parseWarpCoordsForCourse(String coords)
      Extract from the given coords the course.
      Parameters:
      coords - the warp coordinates String in the form "d,d" or "d,d.d" v1.1: clarified "d,d" or "d,d.d", not "d, d" or "d, d.d"
      Returns:
      int representation of the current course
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the course value would be outside the bounds of: 1(inclusive) and less than 9.

    • moveWithinQuadrant

      public void moveWithinQuadrant(Quadrant quadrant, Entity entity, int course, int distance)
      Move the given Entity within the Quadrant, by the course and distance given or until another Entity or edge of the Quadrant would be hit.
      Parameters:
      quadrant - the Quadrant we want the given Entity to move within.
      entity - the Entity want to move within the Quadrant.
      course - the course/direction we wish the given Entity to move within the given Quadrant
      distance - how many sectors in the Quadrant to attempt to move

    • attemptMoveInQuadrant

      public boolean attemptMoveInQuadrant(Quadrant quadrant, Entity entity, XyPair vector)
      Attempt to move an Entity in XyPair direction in this Quadrant. That is, attempt to move the given Entity to the next coordinate in the XyPair direction, in this Quadrant. This is done by adjusting the Entity's position by the given XyPair.

      v1.1 updated: If an edge or other Entity is encountered at the proposed new location, the report for the turn is updated with "HIT THE EDGE OF THIS QUADRANT." or "ENCOUNTERED AN ENTITY: [insert symbol here] EN ROUTE.", and false is returned.

      If the proposed adjustment would be successful the Entity has its position set to the new position and true is returned.

      Parameters:
      quadrant - Quadrant we wish to attempt to move the given Entity in.
      entity - Entity we wish to attempt to move within the given Quadrant
      vector - the adjustment we wish to make ot the Entity's position
      Returns:
      whether the attempted in Quadrant move was successful or not.

    • moveBetweenQuadrants

      public void moveBetweenQuadrants(int course, double distance)
      Moves the Game to a new Quadrant using the given course and distance. Changes which Quadrant the Game is handling using the given course, and given number of quadrants to jump (rounded down).
      Parameters:
      course - the direction of the jump: 1:→, 2:↗, 3:↑, 4:↖, 5:←, 6:↙, 7:↓, 8:↘
      distance - how many quadrants to jump through (we drop the decimal for when jumping between quadrants)

    • attemptMoveBetweenQuadrants

      public boolean attemptMoveBetweenQuadrants(XyPair vector)
      Will attempt to move using a given vector one quadrant and returns a boolean value indicating if it succeeded or failed.
      Parameters:
      vector - vector indicating how we wish to adjust the x and y of our position
      Returns:
      if the attempted move between quadrants was successful

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Returns if the game is still ongoing.
      Returns:
      if the game is still ongoing.
